title: Bit Digital Acquires Enovum Data Centers, Vertically Integrates HPC Business
text: Bit Digital acquired Enovum Data Centers, a high-performance computing datacenter operator headquartered in Montreal, Canada, for total consideration of approximately CAD $62.8 million (approximately USD $46MM). The transaction closed on October 11, 2024. The acquisition was completed on a debt-free, cash-free basis, with CAD $56 million of cash funding and 1.62 million share equivalents issued to key management. The addition of Enovum vertically integrates Bit Digital's HPC business into the colocation services sector, creating potential for significant synergies as Bit Digital may capture additional margin from HPC customers versus hosting them with third party datacenters. Enovum operates a Tier 3 datacenter in Montreal that currently has 4MW of capacity. The company has a pipeline of development sites of more than 280MW, including over 90MW currently under LOI. This strategic acquisition positions Bit Digital to offer end-to-end HPC infrastructure services and improve operational margins through vertical integration.
